title: Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer
content:

*[[Carrier/Legends|Carrier]] *[[Command ship/Legends|Command ship]] |firstuse=Sometime between [[104 ABY|104]] and [[127 ABY]] |retired= |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] *[[Empire-in-exile]] *[[Galactic Alliance Remnant]][[Legacy (2006) 42|''Legacy'' (2006) 42]] *[[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]] }} The '''''Pellaeon''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]''' was a [[Warship/Legends|warship]] design produced by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] to serve as part of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|starfleet]] of the [[Fel Empire]]. It was succeeded by the [[Imperious-class Star Destroyer|''Imperious''-class Star Destroyer]], which was the result of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] forcing the [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]] to reverse engineer an improved version of the ''Pellaeon''-class. ==Characteristics== [[File:Scythefront.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Pellaeon''-class compared to the [[MC140 Scythe-class main battle cruiser|''Scythe''-class main battle cruiser]].]] The ''Pellaeon''-class was named in honor of former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], and drew upon the distinctive and classic wedge-shape of earlier times, made famous by the [[Harrower-class dreadnought|''Harrower'']], [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator'']] and [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperator'']] classes used by the navies of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Ancient Sith Empire]], [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|old Galactic Empire]] respectively. The class was often used as [[Command ship/Legends|command ships]] and combined certain elements from [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnoughts]] like the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class]] and earlier Star Destroyers like the ''Imperial''-class. ===Offensive and defensive systems===
[[File:Legacy8.jpg|thumb|150px|A ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer fighting a [[MC140 Scythe-class main battle cruiser|''Scythe''-class battle cruiser]].]]
The ''Pellaeon''-class was considered one of the most powerful warships in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and noted as having unmatched weapon systems and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] components. With its sleek outline, it presented less of a target to potential enemies, while at the same time optimizing its shield protection. The forward-sloping superstructure of the ''Pellaeon''-class also gave the main gun batteries excellent vantage points, more so than on the Star Destroyers of the previous centuries. The class had slightly more armament than the older [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]]. Some of these guns were of a similar design to the [[XX-9 heavy turbolaser/Legends|XX-9]] turbolaser batteries of the old [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]] The ''Pellaeon''-class also possessed an [[Interdiction field/Legends|interdiction field]], capable of pulling ships out of hyperspace. ===Complement=== [[File:Pellaeon_class_hangar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A hangar on board a ''Pellaeon''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].]] The class had a ventral hangar where starfighters and [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] could land and embark from. [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighters]] would be stored in racks in narrow passages throughout the hangar area. Traffic control centers were located on either side of the main hangar opening, overlooking the area. Its sloping profile allowed the ''Pellaeon''-class to be shorter than the ''Imperial''-class while at the same time be able to carry a decent amount of landing craft and small starships. ===Propulsion systems=== A reactor bulb protrusion was visible outside the ventral hull. It is not known if this was the ship's primary reactor or a forward, secondary one. ===Bridge tower=== The ''Pellaeon''-class had at least one conference room with a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] projector table in the center.[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] Its bridge was styled similar to earlier warship classes such as the ''Venator''-class, with crew pits encircled by a command deck where ship commanders could view the ship's surroundings out of panorama windows.[[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]] ==Known vessels== *''[[Animus]]'' *[[Avenger (Pellaeon-class)|''Avenger'']] *[[Dauntless (Pellaeon-class)|''Dauntless'']] *''[[Fist of the Empire]]'' *[[Imperious (Pellaeon-class)|''Imperious'']] *''[[Iron Sun]]'' *[[Jagged Fel (starship)|''Jagged Fel'']] *[[Relentless (Pellaeon-class)|''Relentless'']] *''[[War Hammer]]'' ==History== [[File:PellaeonvsScythe-LECG.png|thumb|right|250px|A ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer exchanges fire with a MC140 ''Scythe''-class cruiser.]] Thousands of years before the [[Sith–Imperial War]], the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] used a [[Harrower-class dreadnought|capital ship]] similar to the ''Pellaeon''-class during the [[Great Galactic War]].{{TORweb|url=info/timeline/timeline1|text=Pre-game timeline on The Old Republic homepage}} The ''Pellaeon''-class itself began its service over 100 years after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], succeeding the ''Imperial''-class and was noted as going up against the heaviest [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] warships, including the smaller-yet-faster [[MC140 Scythe-class main battle cruiser|''Scythe''-class main battle cruiser]]. After the betrayal of the [[One Sith|new Sith Order]] under [[Darth Krayt]], some of these ships chose to rebel and follow [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] into exile. As of [[137 ABY]], a number of these vessels were kept in orbit and close to his stronghold on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] for protection.[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]] Later in the same year, [[Darth Stryfe]] used a ''Pellaeon''-class vessel to search for Jedi refugees on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. Upon feeling a continued presence after his troops returned empty-handed, he ordered the vessel to destroy the ruins of the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]]. Some Pellaeon's fell into the hands of the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] and were distinguished with its symbol on the sides of their dorsal hulls. Similarly, ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyers loyal to the [[Empire-in-exile]] were distinguished not only by the Imperial seal but by red markings as well.[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]] ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Legacy_destroyer_palleon.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Full blueprints.]] In an interview for [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]], designer and illustrator [[Sean Cooke]] described the design of the ''Pellaeon''-class as a new way to look at [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]]. The original idea entailed not making the vessel incredibly long, like the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], but incredibly tall and with a cathedral-like aspect to the design. The refined design harked back to the original Star Destroyers, but with a "front plow" addition based on the [[Khetanna/Legends|''Khetanna'']], [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] sail barge. While the actual number of guns on the ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer are currently unknown, the ship's entry in [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'']] lists a number of weapon batteries, which according to the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]], represent up to ten weapons of the same type per battery. [[File:Capital_Bombarded.jpg|thumb|200px|[[The Sacking of Coruscant]], with a ''Pellaeon/Harrower'' looming above the cityscape.]] An image of the ''Pellaeon'' was used to represent a [[Harrower-class dreadnought|''Harrower''-class dreadnought]] bombarding Coruscant in ''[[Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant]]'', as the ship's design had not yet been finalized at the time of the video's creation.
